sources.notes: Use the registered-author user page to start a new submission; verify uploaded files and metadata before final submit.
cs.AI covers artificial intelligence; cs.CR covers cryptography and security. Use cs.AI primary and cs.CR secondary only if the final paper framing still matches both.
Use a neutral title, submit the original source, avoid promotion-first behavior, do not solicit votes or comments, and keep comments human-written.
Submission form for title, URL, and optional text/comment after login.
Reddit submit supports link or self posts, titles up to 300 characters, subreddit selection, and captcha fields.
Avoid repeated, unsolicited, mass, or automation-assisted posting. Check subreddit rules before any self-promotional post.
Tweet intent supports prefilled text and enforces the 280-character limit across text, URL, hashtags, and via fields.
X wraps valid URLs with t.co and counts each detected URL as 23 characters, so the publication pack uses reservedUrlChars: 24 including a separator.
Use the LinkedIn home feed share box; posts can include text up to 3000 characters plus a URL preview.

targets.label: arXiv preprint submission
operatorChecks: Confirm the logged-in account is a registered arXiv author and has endorsement if arXiv requests it.
Convert the Markdown paper to the chosen arXiv upload format before final submit.
Use cs.AI as the primary category and cs.CR as secondary only after reviewing the final arXiv category screen.
Preview the compiled PDF and metadata before pressing the final submit button.
postSubmitChecks: Record the arXiv identifier or pending submission ID.
Update launch links in the publication copy before posting to social channels.

targets.label: Hacker News Show HN submission
operatorChecks: Use the original public URL after the paper or repo release is live.
Keep the title neutral and at or below the Hacker News 80-character title target.
Do not solicit votes, comments, or submissions from anyone.
Write any follow-up comments manually; do not paste generated replies.
postSubmitChecks: Stay available to answer questions in the thread without marketing language.

targets.label: Reddit discussion post
operatorChecks: Choose exactly one subreddit after reading that community's current rules and self-promotion policy in the browser.
Do not cross-post the same launch copy to multiple subreddits.
Disclose author affiliation plainly if the chosen subreddit allows the post.
Handle any captcha or subreddit flair requirement manually.
postSubmitChecks: Record the Reddit post URL and selected subreddit.
If removed, do not repost without moderator guidance or materially different context.

targets.label: X launch thread
operatorChecks: Use the publication-pack URL reserve before appending the public paper or repo URL.
Post the second entry as a human-authored reply, not as an automated batch.
Do not claim external Mem0/Zep scores unless strict evidence has passed.
postSubmitChecks: Check that the thread links to the final public artifact, not a local path.

targets.label: LinkedIn launch post
operatorChecks: Paste the final public URL into the share box and wait for the link preview.
Keep the post within LinkedIn's 3000-character text limit.
Do not claim external Mem0/Zep scores unless strict evidence has passed.
postSubmitChecks: Verify the preview points at the public paper or repo URL.
